---
title: "SAS → R"
subtitle: "Becoming fluent in R as a working biostatistician"
---

## What this track covers

I program in SAS daily. R is where the field is moving — pharmaverse, tidyverse, regulatory submissions, and HEOR work all increasingly happen in R. This track is the applied path from "I can read R" to "I'd reach for R first."

## Sections

### [R Fundamentals](r-fundamentals/index.qmd)
Data types, environments, scoping, the things that trip up SAS programmers when they first hit R. The "why does this work this way" entries.

### [dplyr & tidyverse](dplyr-tidyverse/index.qmd)
The core data manipulation grammar. Most of my Week 2–8 R plan lives here — verbs, joins, pivots, list-columns.

### [pharmaverse](pharmaverse/index.qmd)
The clinical R stack: `admiral` for ADaM, `xportr` for transport files, `metacore` for specs, `tfrmt` for tables. This is where R earns its place against SAS in regulated work.

### [SAS vs R patterns](sas-vs-r-patterns/index.qmd)
Side-by-side translations of patterns I use constantly: `proc sort` + `first.` / `last.`, `proc transpose`, `retain`, merge logic, macro variables. Aimed at SAS programmers — including future me.

## Current focus

Week 2 of my 26-week plan: dplyr core verbs (`select`, `filter`, `mutate`, `arrange`, `summarize`, `group_by`).
